text.title: go-concurrency Failure Modes
text.summary: Five antipatterns each with symptom / root-cause / fix.

antipattern.description: Generator goroutine without a ctx.Done() exit: `go func() { for i := 0; ; i++ { ch <- i } }()`.
antipattern.symptom: goleak reports a leaked goroutine at test teardown; memory grows on long-running processes.
antipattern.root-cause: Infinite loop with no exit signal; even when ch is no longer read, the goroutine blocks on send forever.
antipattern.fix: Add `select { case ch <- i: case <-ctx.Done(): return }`. Accept ctx as a parameter.

antipattern.description: Concurrent write to a plain map without a mutex: `go func() { cache[key]++ }()`.
antipattern.symptom: `go test -race` flags `WARNING: DATA RACE` on the map access; production crashes with "concurrent map writes" panic.
antipattern.root-cause: Go maps are not goroutine-safe; concurrent writes crash the runtime.
antipattern.fix: Wrap with sync.RWMutex or use sync.Map for write-once + many-reads. Run -race to verify.

antipattern.description: Calling `wg.Add(1)` inside the goroutine body: `go func() { wg.Add(1); defer wg.Done(); ... }()`.
antipattern.symptom: `wg.Wait()` returns before all goroutines finished; downstream observes a half-baked result.
antipattern.root-cause: Add race: Wait can be called before the goroutine has scheduled its Add.
antipattern.fix: Move `wg.Add(1)` to the line before `go func()`. Inside the goroutine, only `defer wg.Done()`.

antipattern.description: Closing a channel from a goroutine other than the writer.
antipattern.symptom: Sporadic `panic: send on closed channel` during shutdown; rare under low load, reproducible under stress.
antipattern.root-cause: Violates channel-ownership rule: only the writer knows when no more sends are coming.
antipattern.fix: Move `close(ch)` into the writer goroutine. If multiple writers exist, use a `sync.Once` and an explicit "done" channel pattern, or refactor to a single owner.

antipattern.description: `select` without a `<-ctx.Done()` case in a long-lived loop.
antipattern.symptom: Graceful shutdown hangs indefinitely; SIGTERM kills the process after the deadline.
antipattern.root-cause: No way for ctx cancellation to interrupt the select.
antipattern.fix: Always include `case <-ctx.Done(): return ctx.Err()` (or break) in every select that runs in a loop.
